name: Sketch
description: Template for 1-5 minute comedy sketches — premise, game of the scene, escalation, blow.

subsections.name: Idea Wizard
subsections.description: Not sure what your sketch is about yet? Use the Idea Wizard to find the ONE unusual thing your sketch explores and the game it plays.

fields.label: Comedy Style
fields.placeholder: e.g., Absurdist, Satire, Character piece, Parody...

fields.label: Initial Idea
fields.placeholder: Describe the funny idea — what's the ONE unusual thing? (e.g., a hostage negotiator who takes everything literally)

fields.label: Target Runtime
fields.placeholder: e.g., 2 minutes, 4 minutes

fields.label: Tone
fields.placeholder: e.g., Deadpan, Manic, Dry, Grounded-absurd...

subsections.chat_system_prompt: You are an expert sketch-comedy development partner trained in the UCB tradition. Help the user find and sharpen their sketch premise. Every sketch needs exactly ONE unusual thing (the premise) and a repeatable comedic pattern (the game). Push for specificity: who is the voice of reason, what is the first laugh, how does the game heighten? Kill premises with two unrelated weird things — one game per sketch.

subsections.name: Import Sketch
subsections.description: Bring an existing sketch to seed beats, scenes, and the screenplay editor. Paste or upload it, optionally include guidance, and we will align beats and scenes for you.

fields.label: Paste Sketch
fields.placeholder: Paste your sketch here...

fields.label: Guidance (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Any specific guidance for how to align beats and scenes...

subsections.name: Core
subsections.description: Lock the comedic engine of your sketch. A sketch is not a short film: it explores ONE unusual thing through a repeatable game. If you can't state the premise and the game in one line each, the sketch isn't ready.

cards.label: Premise (The Unusual Thing)
cards.placeholder: The ONE thing in this world that is absurd, exaggerated or wrong. Everything else stays normal.

cards.label: Game of the Scene
cards.placeholder: The repeatable comedic pattern. 'Every time X happens, Y responds by...' If it can't repeat, it's not a game.

cards.label: Base Reality
cards.placeholder: The grounded, recognizable situation BEFORE the unusual thing appears. The more normal, the funnier the break.

cards.label: Voice of Reason
cards.placeholder: Who reacts the way a normal person would? The audience surrogate who notices the weird.

cards.label: Why Today?
cards.placeholder: Why is this scene happening NOW? What kicks the unusual thing into motion today of all days?

cards.label: Heightening Logic
cards.placeholder: The internal rule that makes each escalation bigger but still consistent. Absurdity must escalate along ONE axis.

cards.label: Stakes
cards.placeholder: What does the straight character stand to lose if the weirdness continues? Small stakes played big.

cards.label: Comedic Target
cards.placeholder: If satirical: who or what is this really about? (Optional but sharpens every choice.)

cards.label: Unique Element
cards.placeholder: The image, prop, catchphrase or world rule that makes this sketch unmistakably yours.

subsections.name: Characters
subsections.description: Sketch characters are functions of the game: someone embodies the unusual thing, someone reacts to it. Two or three characters is almost always enough — every extra body dilutes the game.

fields.label: The Unusual Behavior
fields.placeholder: The specific, committed way they embody the premise. They never think they're weird.

fields.label: Blind Spot
fields.placeholder: What they can never see about themselves — the engine of the game.

fields.label: What They Want
fields.placeholder: The mundane goal they pursue with total sincerity despite the absurdity.

fields.label: Dialogue Style
fields.placeholder: How they speak — rhythm, vocabulary, verbal habits. Commit to the bit.

fields.label: Core Trait
fields.placeholder: Grounded, reasonable, with a normal goal the weirdness keeps blocking.

fields.label: Why They Stay
fields.placeholder: The reason they can't just leave the scene — obligation, politeness, desperation.

fields.label: What They Want
fields.placeholder: The simple thing they came here to do.

fields.label: Function in the Game
fields.placeholder: How they feed the game: escalate it, validate the absurd character, or raise stakes.

subsections.name: Beats
subsections.description: Map the sketch structure: establish a believable base reality, land the first laugh, name the game, then heighten it in escalating beats along one axis — and get out at the peak. No third-act redemption, no lesson: the blow ends the sketch the moment the game can't go higher.

cards.label: Base Reality (Open)
cards.placeholder: Ground us fast in a recognizable situation. Who, where, and the normal goal — in seconds, not minutes.

cards.label: First Laugh (The Unusual Thing Appears)
cards.placeholder: The premise lands for the first time. The earlier the first laugh, the better the sketch.

cards.label: Game Established
cards.placeholder: The pattern repeats and the audience learns the rules: 'oh, THIS is what this scene is.'

cards.label: Heightening 1
cards.placeholder: Same game, bigger. Raise intensity, scope or cost while staying consistent with the heightening logic.

cards.label: Heightening 2
cards.placeholder: Bigger again. The straight character's resistance peaks; the absurd character doubles down.

cards.label: Break / Twist (optional)
cards.placeholder: A reframe that recharges the game right before the end: a reveal, a flip of who's weird, a new victim.

cards.label: The Blow (Out)
cards.placeholder: The last, biggest beat of the game — then CUT. Get out at the peak laugh; never linger to explain.

cards.label: Button / Tag (optional)
cards.placeholder: One final quick beat after the blow — a single extra laugh, five seconds max.

subsections.name: Scene Wizard
subsections.description: Most sketches are ONE scene playing one game. Use multiple scenes only for cutaway or escalating-location formats. The Scene Wizard builds your scene list from the beats.

wizard_config.guidance_default: Construct scenes for a comedy sketch. Default to ONE scene that plays a single game from base reality to blow. If multiple scenes, each must heighten the SAME game — never introduce a second unrelated weird thing. Front-load the first laugh, escalate along one axis, and end at the peak beat with no resolution or moral.
wizard_config.beat_mapping_strategy: Map the sketch beats in order: base reality, first laugh, game established, heightenings, optional break, blow, optional button. In a one-scene sketch all beats live in that scene as an escalation ladder. In multi-scene sketches, each scene owns one or two rungs of the ladder.

subsections.name: Individual Scenes
subsections.description: Craft the scene as an escalation ladder. In a sketch, the 'turning point' is a heightening of the game, the 'climax' is the blow, and the 'fallout' is the button. Everything serves the game — cut any line that doesn't feed it.

fields.label: Arena
fields.placeholder: The grounded location. One arena per sketch unless the format IS the location jump.

fields.label: First Laugh
fields.placeholder: Where the unusual thing first lands in this scene.

fields.label: Straight Character's Goal
fields.placeholder: The simple thing they're trying to accomplish while the game blocks them.

fields.label: Subtext
fields.placeholder: What the characters won't say: the politeness, denial or desperation that keeps them in the scene.

fields.label: Heightening
fields.placeholder: The beat where the game jumps a level.

fields.label: Peak Resistance
fields.placeholder: The straight character's last attempt to restore normality.

fields.label: The Blow
fields.placeholder: The biggest beat of the game — the out.

fields.label: Button / Tag
fields.placeholder: Optional single extra laugh after the blow.

fields.label: Hand-off (multi-scene only)
fields.placeholder: How this scene tees up the next rung of the escalation ladder.
